Overview

Address
37 SOUTH ELLIS STREET, CAMILLA, GA 31730

Government (county)

1979-07-01
Setting
Rural
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.18 3.55 3.45 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.63 0.62 0.69 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.84 0.79 0.91 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.71 2.14 1.86 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.47 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.88
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.34
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.13
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
42.5%
annual
RN turnover
48.0%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.26
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.91
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-05-16 23 17 6 273 1 273
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-05-11 6 6 0 72 1 72
Total weighted health score
222.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (39)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

35 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
2 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. J 2026-01-08 2026-02-20
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2026-01-08 2026-02-20
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. J 2026-01-08 2026-02-20
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2026-01-08 2026-02-20
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. E 2025-09-10 2025-12-13
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2025-09-10 2025-12-22
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2025-05-16 2025-06-19
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. D 2025-05-16 2025-06-19
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2025-05-16 2025-06-19
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. D 2025-05-16 2025-06-19
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-05-16 2025-06-19
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-05-16 2025-06-19
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-05-16 2025-06-19
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-05-16 2025-06-19
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-05-16 2025-06-19
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. D 2025-05-16 2025-06-19
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2025-05-16 2025-06-19
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-05-16 2025-06-19
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. F 2025-05-16 2025-06-19
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-05-16 2025-06-19
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2025-05-16 2025-06-19
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. E 2025-05-16 2025-06-19
0887 Educate residents and staff on COVID-19 vaccination, offer the COVID-19 vaccine to eligible residents and staff after education, and properly document each resident and staff member's vaccination status. F 2025-05-16 2025-06-19
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2023-05-11 2023-06-21
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. G 2023-05-11 2023-06-21
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-05-11 2023-06-21
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. G 2023-05-11 2023-06-21
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2023-05-11 2023-06-21
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2023-05-11 2023-06-21
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2021-07-30 2021-09-24
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2021-07-30 2021-09-24
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2021-07-30 2021-09-24
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2021-07-30 2021-09-24
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2021-07-30 2021-09-24
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2021-07-30 2021-09-24
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2021-07-30 2021-09-24
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2021-07-30 2021-09-24
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2021-07-30 2021-09-24
0914 Provide bedrooms that don't allow residents to see each other when privacy is needed. E 2021-07-30 2021-09-24
